message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Process update: consensus before push">
PROCESS UPDATE from the project owner:

The Atomic Green Step pipeline is being reordered. Reviewer consensus now happens BEFORE push, not after:

1. Write code (smallest meaningful change)
2. cargo fmt --check (fix if needed)
3. cargo clippy -- -D warnings (fix if needed)
4. cargo test (fix if needed)
5. git commit (locally — do NOT push yet)
6. All 9 agents review and reach consensus
7. If concerns raised → driver fixes, re-runs steps 2-5, re-seeks consensus
8. Once 9/9 consensus → git push
9. gh run list --limit 1 — verify CI green (team can start next task while CI runs)

This avoids the 2.5 minute CI wait being in the feedback loop. Reviewers can review the local commit, and any fixes happen before push.

Carson: apply this updated flow starting now for Task #4.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Understood. Consensus before push makes sense -- it tightens the feedback loop and avoids pushing code that might need revision. I'll review local commits the same way I've been reviewing pushed ones. Standing by for Task #4.
